user_message: Just came out of QBR preparation with Alpine Ski House. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: QBR preparation Discussion - Alpine Ski House
Date: 2024-01-04
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House), Marcus Williams (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on QBR preparation from our last session.

[00:08:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:11:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): Let me add some context -- we've been working on technical validation and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:15:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:20:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:24:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

handoffs.condition: architecture sketch needed from technical signals

Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF44-L failure mode (Migration-architecture module output template doesn't accommodate iterative deep)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
